name: Plan-Do-Check-Act

problemSignals.description: Changes are made without a baseline, prediction, or learning loop.
Corrective actions recur without standardized follow-through.

requiredEvidence.description: Operational definition, baseline measure, target, and observation window.
A bounded change, predicted effect, risks, and rollback condition.

assumptions: The change can be bounded and observed
The check interval is long enough to detect the predicted effect

deterministicSteps.action: Record baseline, target, hypothesis, change scope, safeguards, and check criteria.
Apply only the bounded trial and compare observed measures with the plan using the declared criteria.
Classify the result as standardize, adapt, abandon, or repeat and preserve the evidence for the next cycle.

outputs.description: Bounded improvement hypothesis and evaluation contract.
Evidence-linked proposal for standardization or another cycle.

whenAll: Baseline and target are operationally defined
Confounders or sample size limit attribution
The change and check criteria were altered during the cycle

contraindications: The proposed action is irreversible without prior risk controls
No measurable target or bounded trial can be defined

contract: Bind each phase to one cycle identity
Do not convert the Act phase into automatic adoption authority
